payload.reason: Victor updated Builder Console task status with grounded evidence and governance approval.

request.taskTitle: Replace fixed decay factor with saturation and temperature state
request.query: For the governed automation task "Replace fixed decay factor with saturation and temperature state" in phase "Thermodynamic Decay Foundation", choose the single highest-value next safe action Victor should take now. Prefer direct advancement of the task. If that is not justified, choose the most concrete unblock action that would create governed progress. Return guidance that supports exactly one governed action or one explicit no-action veto with a named blocker. Do not recommend passive observation, generic monitoring, or "continue current work" unless you also identify the exact blocker or missing artifact.

semanticNodeLabels: We have uncovered how each system functions individually, but direct integration points are not implemented in the provided code. For example, FailSafe’s extension code does not call into AgentMesh or Agent OS. No connectors (APIs or adapters) exist for QoreLogic ↔ IATP or kernel. The biggest gaps are at the interfaces: translating actions/intents across systems. Further work would involve designing APIs (REST, RPC, CLI) so one system can subscribe to the other’s events, or adopting common identity formats. Additionally, running Agent OS on FailSafe code would require a language bridge. These would be next investigative steps
Replace fixed decay factor with saturation and temperature state
Create fresh Victor and Builder UI shell
Single evaluate() method handles both ContentAdmissionRequest and ActionExecutionRequest
Prepare unattended 30m heartbeat operation with safe stop conditions
Repeated blocks or missing eligible work stop the unattended loop cleanly instead of churning.
Policy schema supports both content trust tiers and action risk levels
recommendedNextActions: Implement memory records store saturation instead of a fixed decay factor

before.title: Replace fixed decay factor with saturation and temperature state
before.description: Refactor memory records so unresolvedness is represented through saturation and derived context temperature instead of a single opaque decay factor.
acceptance: Memory records store saturation instead of a fixed decay factor.
Context temperature and effective lambda are derived from saturation.
Existing callers can inspect thermodynamic state without reconstructing it externally.
